sentence_reviews.text: Model registries and MLOps systems version models, adapters, datasets, evaluations, approvals, lineage, deployment targets, and rollback state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support data-system versioning/operations, governance, adapters, and merged models separately; no direct MLOps registry source covers the full list.
sentence_reviews.notes: Add an MLOps/model-registry reference for exact support.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps is to connect Object storage, sharding & checkpoints, Workflow, cluster & job orchestration to downstream capabilities including Large language models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support operational systems and governance broadly; downstream LLM linkage is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible atlas positioning, not direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources show LLM adaptation/model composition and governance relevance, but not registry as an LLM substrate directly.

sentence_reviews.text: combined with Object storage, sharding & checkpoints

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Object storage, sharding & checkpoints, Workflow, cluster & job orchestration.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support operational and governance risks generally; exact failure-mode list is template-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Numerical behavior is not specific to registries; dependency list is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Model registry, versioning & MLOps / LLMOps turns Evaluation-driven development & release gates into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.

edge_reviews.notes: Registries naturally depend on stored artifacts and checkpoint metadata.
MLOps registries commonly integrate with workflow/deployment orchestration, though the packet evidence is broad.
Lineage and monitoring concerns make this a plausible common dependency.
Signing is plausible for artifact integrity and audit trails.
Composed artifacts plausibly need registry/versioning support.
Optional data-pipeline linkage is plausible but indirect.
Release-gate linkage is plausible but not directly cited.
